description:
A developer is tidying up application logs so that only critical messages 
    (WARNING and ERROR) are kept for later inspection.

    1. A text log already exists at  
       /home/user/workspace/app/logs/build_2023-09-01.log

    2. Create a **new** filtered log named  
       /home/user/workspace/app/logs/critical_2023-09-01.log  
       containing **only** those lines from the source log whose log-level token
       is either “WARNING” or “ERROR”.  
       • Preserve the exact order and text of the chosen lines.  
       • Do not add, remove, or re-wrap whitespace.  
       • The file must end with a single newline character.

    3. Create a simple one-line summary counter file called  
       /home/user/workspace/app/logs/critical_2023-09-01.count  
       The format is strictly:  
           WARNING=<number_of_warning_lines>,ERROR=<number_of_error_lines>  
       Example (do **NOT** hard-code this example):  
           WARNING=7,ERROR=3  
       The file must contain **exactly one line** with no trailing spaces or
       extra newline.

    4. Save a plain-text record of the exact commands you used (max 3 commands) 
       in /home/user/workspace/app/logs/filter_commands.log  
       • Each command on its own line.  
       • Do not include the command prompts, only what you typed.  

    The automated test will confirm:  
       • critical_2023-09-01.log exists and holds **exactly** the WARNING/ERROR
         lines in their original order.  
       • critical_2023-09-01.count follows the precise 
         “WARNING=<int>,ERROR=<int>” format and the numbers match the filtered
         log.  
       • filter_commands.log contains 1–3 non-blank lines.  

    You do not have root access and should only rely on standard GNU/Linux
    user-space utilities that are available in a minimal distribution (e.g.
    grep, wc, printf, cat, etc.).  
    Keep the solution to at most three shell commands.
truth:
The baseline file already present before the agent starts:

    Path: /home/user/workspace/app/logs/build_2023-09-01.log  
    Contents (including final newline):
        2023-09-01 10:01:02 INFO [Init] Application started
        2023-09-01 10:01:03 WARNING [Config] Missing optional setting "theme"
        2023-09-01 10:01:04 ERROR [Database] Connection refused
        2023-09-01 10:02:00 INFO [Heartbeat] Alive
        2023-09-01 10:03:12 ERROR [Auth] Invalid credentials
        2023-09-01 10:04:45 INFO [Shutdown] Application stopped

    Expected artefacts **after** the agent completes:

    1. /home/user/workspace/app/logs/critical_2023-09-01.log  
       Exact contents:
        2023-09-01 10:01:03 WARNING [Config] Missing optional setting "theme"
        2023-09-01 10:01:04 ERROR [Database] Connection refused
        2023-09-01 10:03:12 ERROR [Auth] Invalid credentials
       (followed by a single newline)

    2. /home/user/workspace/app/logs/critical_2023-09-01.count  
       Exact contents (single line, no trailing newline after the final
       character):
        WARNING=1,ERROR=2

    3. /home/user/workspace/app/logs/filter_commands.log  
       The grader will only verify that the file exists and has between
       1-3 non-empty lines (it will not check their exact text).

    Directory /home/user/workspace/app/logs/ pre-exists and is writable by the
    regular user.  No other files will be present in that directory prior to
    task execution.
